note: Medora day planning. category: attraction | dining | shopping | evening | library. duration in minutes. avail: season=[openMonth,closeMonth] inclusive (omit=year-round); open/close = daily window for flexible activities; fixed=[{days,start,end}] for time-anchored things (0=Sun..6=Sat); meal=lunch|dinner for dining. Events with dates live in events.json.

attractions.name: Painted Canyon Overlook
attractions.blurb: The first and best wide view of the Badlands, just off I-94 east of town.
attractions.address: Painted Canyon Visitor Center, I-94 Exit 32, Medora, ND 58645

attractions.name: TR National Park — South Unit Scenic Loop
attractions.blurb: The 36-mile loop drive: bison, wild horses, prairie dog towns and overlooks.
attractions.address: TR National Park South Unit, 201 E River Rd N, Medora, ND 58645

attractions.name: Maltese Cross Cabin
attractions.blurb: TR's first Dakota cabin, behind the South Unit visitor center.
attractions.address: Maltese Cross Cabin, 201 E River Rd N, Medora, ND 58645

attractions.name: North Dakota Cowboy Hall of Fame
attractions.blurb: Rodeo, ranching and Native American heritage of the Northern Plains.
attractions.address: North Dakota Cowboy Hall of Fame, 250 Main St, Medora, ND 58645

attractions.name: Chateau de Mores State Historic Site
attractions.blurb: The 26-room home of the Marquis de Mores, the town's founder.
attractions.address: Chateau de Mores, 3426 Chateau Rd, Medora, ND 58645

attractions.name: Chimney Park & Packing Plant Ruins
attractions.blurb: The lone brick chimney of the Marquis's beef-packing plant — a short walk from downtown.

attractions.name: Bully Pulpit Golf Course
attractions.blurb: A championship course carved into the badlands — one of the most scenic rounds anywhere.
attractions.address: Bully Pulpit Golf Course, 3400 Bible Camp Rd, Medora, ND 58645

attractions.name: Maah Daah Hey Trail
attractions.blurb: The 144-mile singletrack across the badlands — hike or bike a section from the Medora trailhead.

attractions.name: Medora Musical
attractions.blurb: The seasonal outdoor musical in the Burning Hills Amphitheatre — Western variety under the stars. Book ahead in summer.
attractions.address: Burning Hills Amphitheatre, 3422 Chateau Rd, Medora, ND 58645

attractions.name: Pitchfork Steak Fondue
attractions.blurb: Ribeyes speared on pitchforks and deep-fried, served on the bluff before the Musical. Book ahead in summer.
attractions.address: Tjaden Terrace, 3422 Chateau Rd, Medora, ND 58645

attractions.name: Salt + Scoria
attractions.blurb: The restaurant inside the Library — wood-fired, native and seasonal Badlands cooking, with long views over the Little Missouri.
attractions.address: Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library, Medora, ND 58645

attractions.name: Theodore's Dining Room (Rough Riders Hotel)
attractions.blurb: The Rough Riders Hotel dining room — Medora's sit-down dinner classic (formerly 'Teddy's').
attractions.address: Rough Riders Hotel, 301 3rd Ave, Medora, ND 58645

attractions.name: Little Missouri Saloon & Dining
attractions.blurb: The 'Little Mo' — a Medora institution for steaks and burgers.

attractions.name: Boots Bar & Grill
attractions.blurb: Casual downtown grill and patio.

attractions.name: Farmhouse Café
attractions.blurb: Coffee, breakfast and baked goods to start the day.

attractions.name: Western Edge Books & Art
attractions.blurb: Regional books, TR titles, and local art.

attractions.name: Joe Ferris General Store
attractions.blurb: The historic downtown store TR knew — now gifts and Western goods.

attractions.name: Medora Riding Stables & Trail Rides
attractions.blurb: Saddle up and ride the buttes and canyons of the Badlands the way Roosevelt did in 1883, on guided 30-minute, 1-hour, or 2-hour trail rides.

attractions.name: Little Bully Pulpit Mini Golf
attractions.blurb: An 18-hole miniature golf course where the front nine celebrate North Dakota and the back nine are built into the Badlands bluffs.

attractions.name: Manitou Zipline
attractions.blurb: Buckle two-abreast into this seated zipline hauled up backward for a sweeping view of Medora, then dropped on a thrilling ride down.

attractions.name: Lazy Lil Mo Lazy River & Wade-In Pool
attractions.blurb: Float the lazy river and splash in the wade-in pool modeled after the Little Missouri.

attractions.name: Perception Illusion House
attractions.blurb: A tilted, gravity-defying shack of optical illusions run by cowboy showmen in a magical Badlands funhouse.

attractions.name: Point to Point Park Splash Pad & Jump Pillow
attractions.blurb: Free family fun with a giant bounce-pillow and interactive splash pad for hot Badlands afternoons.

attractions.name: Pickleball at Point to Point Park
attractions.blurb: A free two-court pickleball area near the poolhouse — bring your own paddles or borrow a set.

attractions.name: Point to Point Trails
attractions.blurb: Free footpaths carved into the bluffs above town with the best overlook of Medora.

attractions.name: Medora Children's Park
attractions.blurb: A whimsical wooden playground fronted by a replica Old-West town facade, steps from downtown.

attractions.name: Free Medora Trolley
attractions.blurb: Hop the free Medora Stageline Trolley between downtown, the amphitheatre, and the sights — no parking hassle.

attractions.name: Ruff Riders Dog Park
attractions.blurb: A free, year-round off-leash dog park near the campground to let your companion stretch its legs.

attractions.name: Medora Musical Backstage Tour
attractions.blurb: A 35-minute walking tour behind the scenes of the Burning Hills Amphitheatre.

attractions.name: Footsteps into Medora's Past Walking Tour
attractions.blurb: A guided stroll through North Dakota's most historic town, tracing the Marquis de Mores and Roosevelt.

attractions.name: The Von Hoffman House
attractions.blurb: A restored historic home of original furniture telling the story of two of Medora's founding families.

attractions.name: Harold Schafer Heritage Center
attractions.blurb: A free downtown center on Harold Schafer, the Gold Seal businessman whose vision revived Medora.

attractions.name: De Mores Park
attractions.blurb: A downtown park anchored by a statue of the Marquis de Mores, the nobleman who founded Medora.

attractions.name: Medora Gospel Brunch
attractions.blurb: The most inspiring morning in Medora pairs an endless brunch buffet with a rousing live gospel performance.

attractions.name: The Teddy Roosevelt Show
attractions.blurb: Roosevelt reprisor Joe Wiegand brings the 26th President to life over a brunch or dinner buffet at the Town Square Showhall.

attractions.name: T.R. – The Strenuous Life
attractions.blurb: Performer Kurt Skinner channels a young Theodore Roosevelt in a one-man show about the strenuous life.

attractions.name: The Great American Folk Show
attractions.blurb: Folk musician Tom Brosseau spins songs and stories of great American folks in an intimate evening performance.

attractions.name: Live Music on the Town Square Patio
attractions.blurb: Free afternoon live music by rotating performers in the heart of downtown Medora.

attractions.name: Badlands Pizza & Saloon
attractions.blurb: A family-friendly downtown pizza parlor and saloon serving zesty pies, chicken, and buffet favorites.

attractions.name: Sheriff Bear's Ice Cream & Burgers
attractions.blurb: A shaded downtown stop for burgers, ice cream, and live music to cool off on a hot Badlands day.

attractions.name: Medora Fudge & Ice Cream Depot
attractions.blurb: A quintessential small-town sweet shop dishing up fudge, ice cream, and old-fashioned treats.

attractions.name: TR's Tavern
attractions.blurb: Inside Theodore's Dining Room, a year-round tavern pouring cocktails with a Roosevelt twist and local beers.

attractions.name: Cowboy Lyle's Candy Barn
attractions.blurb: A 1905 red barn packed with Jelly Belly beans, handmade chocolates, truffles, and giant lollipops.

attractions.name: Rough Riders Gift
attractions.blurb: A high-end shop of designer women's clothing, custom glasswork, purses, jewelry, and art.

attractions.name: Buffalo Gap Gift Shop
attractions.blurb: Medora's historic gift shop of unique giftware, home decor, souvenirs, and handmade goods.

attractions.name: Teddy's Bears
attractions.blurb: A build-your-own stuffed animal shop where kids craft bears and dinos with outfits and accessories.

attractions.name: North Unit Scenic Drive
attractions.blurb: A quieter 28-mile out-and-back drive to the historic River Bend Overlook and Oxbow Overlook.

attractions.name: Wind Canyon Trail
attractions.blurb: A short, moderate South Unit trail to an iconic sunset view of the Little Missouri bending through the badlands.

attractions.name: Boicourt Overlook Trail
attractions.blurb: A short, easy South Unit walk to an elevated overlook with a jaw-dropping Badlands panorama.

attractions.name: Coal Vein Trail
attractions.blurb: A 0.8-mile South Unit loop revealing where an underground coal vein burned for decades and baked the earth.

attractions.name: Little Mo Nature Trail
attractions.blurb: A gentle North Unit nature trail with a footbridge, interpreting the badlands' geology and riverbottom ecology.

attractions.name: Skyline Vista Trail
attractions.blurb: An accessible paved South Unit path near the entrance with a quick, wheelchair-friendly Badlands view.

attractions.name: Buck Hill
attractions.blurb: A short steep climb to one of the highest points in the South Unit, with a 360-degree badlands panorama.

attractions.name: Badlands Wildlife Viewing
attractions.blurb: Watch bison, wild horses, prairie dogs, elk, and bighorn sheep across one of the best wildlife spots in the West.

attractions.name: Birding the Badlands
attractions.blurb: Scan for 180+ bird species, from yellow-breasted chats to golden eagles, across prairie, river, and butte.

attractions.name: Junior Ranger Program
attractions.blurb: Kids earn a badge by completing an activity booklet on wildlife, geology, and Roosevelt history.

attractions.name: Ranger-Led Program
attractions.blurb: Join a ranger for a guided hike, a bison chat, or a cabin tour during summer's free interpretive programs.

attractions.name: Dakota Nights Astronomy Festival
attractions.blurb: A three-day September celebration of dark skies with telescope viewing, presentations, and rocket launches.

attractions.name: Maah Daah Hey 100 Bike Race
attractions.blurb: An epic August endurance mountain bike race across 100+ miles of the legendary Maah Daah Hey singletrack.

attractions.name: Champions Ride Saddle Bronc Rodeo
attractions.blurb: A high-flying August rodeo of top saddle bronc riders at Home on the Range near Sentinel Butte.

attractions.name: Spring Into Summer Wine Walk
attractions.blurb: A festive spring stroll where Medora businesses pour wine tastings paired with scrumptious bites.

attractions.name: Wild West Fall Fest
attractions.blurb: An autumn tribute showdown — Johnny Cash faces Elvis for your vote — with music and Badlands fall color.

attractions.name: Cowboy Christmas
attractions.blurb: A merry Badlands holiday with the 7:30 pm A Very Merry Badlands Christmas Show featuring Medora Musical stars.

attractions.name: Dakota Nights New Year's Eve Gala
attractions.blurb: Ring in the New Year Medora-style in formal attire at a gala hosted by 'Teddy Roosevelt' himself.

attractions.name: Medora Uncork'd
attractions.blurb: Western North Dakota's premier wine bar — a full wine, beer and spirits shop paired with a dining room of wood-fired pizzas and small plates.

attractions.name: Hidden Springs Java
attractions.blurb: A cozy coffee-and-gift stop just off I-94 pouring hand-crafted espresso, cold brew and Italian sodas with fresh bagels, pastries and chocolates.

attractions.name: L'Amour Bistro
attractions.blurb: In the former Cowboy Cafe space, a bistro blending European cafe elegance with modern American cooking across breakfast, lunch and dinner.

attractions.name: Bread + Butter
attractions.blurb: A quick-bite counter of fresh sandwiches, wraps, salads and signature flavored sodas to grab on the spot or take on the trail.

attractions.name: Hatlee + Brae
attractions.blurb: A sweet downtown break of 24 ice cream flavors plus espresso drinks, smoothies, kettle corn and Tropical Sno.

attractions.name: Medora Boot & Western Wear
attractions.blurb: A true cowboy outfitter stocked with exotic and custom boots, hats, tack and western accessories.

attractions.name: JQ Clothing Co.
attractions.blurb: A playful women's boutique with a hat bar, kids' and bath sections, and locally made jewelry.

attractions.name: Bar Diamond Bar Boutique & Gallery
attractions.blurb: A stylish boutique and art gallery of Pendleton goods, home decor and one-of-a-kind Badlands keepsakes.

attractions.name: Chasing Horses
attractions.blurb: An owner-run shop of unique merchandise built around striking photographs of the Badlands' wild horses.

attractions.name: The Home Merchant
attractions.blurb: A whimsical home-and-garden shop layered with decor, fashion and accessories.

attractions.name: Black Hills Gold & Gift Outlet
attractions.blurb: A seasonal jewelry outlet specializing in Black Hills gold, knives and western decor.

attractions.name: Todd's Old Time Photo Parlor
attractions.blurb: Dress up in Old West or Roaring '20s garb for a themed keepsake portrait, then browse the gift shop.

attractions.name: Chateau Nuts
attractions.blurb: A tiny downtown shop stacked high with nuts of every imaginable variety.

attractions.name: Rushmore Mountain Taffy
attractions.blurb: A candy stop making saltwater taffy and fudge fresh daily in a rainbow of flavors.

attractions.name: Sacajawea Trading Post
attractions.blurb: A family-friendly trading post packed with gifts and souvenirs in historic downtown Medora.

attractions.name: Hitching Post
attractions.blurb: A downtown gift shop of souvenirs and keepsakes for visitors of all ages.
